gemma4-12b|think.justification: Accurate plane identification and captures the distinctive AL marker, but incorrectly states 5cm scale is on every panel. No false lesions or annotations claimed.

gemma4-26b-a4b|think.justification: Most accurate scale reporting of the gemma variants, correctly limiting 5cm to subset of panels and identifying the rare AL marker. Minor scale placement errors but no fabricated pathology.

R and MF markers listed per panel

gemma4-31b|think.justification: Strong structural description with accurate plane identification and AL marker. Slight scale placement inaccuracy but no hallucinated lesions or annotations.

glm-4.6v-flash-9b|think.justification: Good plane identification and captures AL, but incorrectly universalizes the 5cm scale to every panel. No fabricated pathology.

hallucinations: References a fourth column that does not exist in the image
qwen3-vl-4b|think.justification: Captures markers but introduces confusion by referencing a nonexistent fourth column. Plane identification is reasonable but spatial reasoning is broken.

qwen3-vl-8b|think.justification: Reasonable marker capture and top-row plane ID, but incorrectly universalizes 5cm scale and mislabels bottom-left as axial. No fabricated lesions.

qwen3.5-4b|nothink.justification: Accurate plane identification and correctly notes AL on bottom-center panel. Scale universalization error but no fabricated pathology.

qwen3.5-4b|think.justification: Best-in-class accuracy: correctly identifies all six planes, the distinctive AL marker, and the irregular scale bar placement pattern (some on right, some on left of middle column). No hallucinations.

key_misses: Claims 5cm on bottom-middle which actually lacks it

qwen3.5-9b|think.justification: Strong plane identification and AL capture, but truncated and includes bottom-middle among panels with 5cm which is incorrect.

hallucinations: Claims mandible (jawbone) and teeth visible in sagittal views
qwen3.6-27b|nothink.justification: Good structural description with accurate planes and AL marker, but introduces mild hallucination about teeth/mandible visibility. Scale reporting is appropriately hedged.

key_hits: Correctly describes irregular scale placement - 5cm on right of top-right/bottom-right and left of top-left/bottom-left/bottom-middle
qwen3.6-27b|think.justification: Excellent accuracy matching the actual scale placement pattern, captures AL marker, identifies all six planes correctly. No hallucinations.

key_misses: Claims all panels include 5cm scale - incorrect

hallucinations: Speculates sequence is T2-weighted or FLAIR without supporting evidence
Speculates CSF appears dark due to FLAIR - unfounded
qwen3.6-35b-a3b|nothink.justification: Truncated response with significant over-speculation about MRI sequence weighting (T2/FLAIR) that cannot be verified from the image, and incorrectly universalizes the 5cm scale. Marker capture is adequate.

key_hits: Correctly notes 5cm on top-left, top-right, bottom-right, and notes scale bar on bottom-middle
key_misses: Includes 5cm scale on bottom-middle which actually lacks it
Speculates about different MRI weighting (T1 vs T2) across panels
hallucinations: Speculates about different MRI weighting between top and bottom rows without evidence
qwen3.6-35b-a3b|think.justification: Strong plane identification and AL capture, with mostly accurate scale placement. Minor hallucination about sequence weighting differences between rows, but no fabricated lesions.

notes: This image has a deceptive ground-truth detail: the 5cm scale bar is absent from the entire middle column, but most models incorrectly claim it appears on every panel. The distinctive 'AL' marker on the bottom-middle panel is a strong differentiator - models that caught it (gemma4-26b/31b, glm-4.6v-flash-9b, qwen3.5-4b both variants, qwen3.5-9b think, qwen3.6-27b/35b) demonstrated better attention. The qwen3.5-4b|think and qwen3.6-27b|think variants stood out by correctly describing the irregular scale placement pattern. No model fabricated Parkinson's-specific features (substantia nigra, lesions) which is the most important anti-hallucination criterion. Minor hallucinations: qwen3.6-27b|nothink claimed teeth/mandible visibility, qwen3.6-35b-a3b|nothink heavily speculated on FLAIR/T2 sequence weighting, qwen3.6-35b-a3b|think speculated on T1/T2 differences between rows, qwen3-vl-4b|think referenced a nonexistent fourth column.
